beg: verb. To request the help of someone, often in the form of money.
beige: noun. A colour, variously defined from a pale brown, to a yellow greyish off-white.

bilge: noun. The rounded portion of a ship 's hull, forming a transition between the bottom and the sides.
bill: noun. A written list or inventory. (Now obsolete except in specific senses or set phrases; bill of lading, bill of goods, etc.)
eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
eligible: adjective. Allowed to and meeting the necessary conditions required to participate in or be chosen for something.
gee: interjection. A general exclamation of surprise or frustration.
